We are given the half-cell reaction:  
\[
\text{AgCl}(s) + e^- \rightarrow \text{Ag}(s) + \text{Cl}^-(aq)
\]  
with \( \mu^\circ(\text{AgCl}, s) = -109.71 \, \text{kJ mol}^{-1} \) and \( E^\circ = +0.222 \, \text{V} \).  

The standard Gibbs energy change for the half-cell reaction is  
\[
\Delta G^\circ = -nFE^\circ = -(1)(96485)(0.222) \approx -21.42 \, \text{kJ mol}^{-1}.
\]  
Since \(\Delta G^\circ = \mu^\circ(\text{Ag}) + \mu^\circ(\text{Cl}^-) - \mu^\circ(\text{AgCl})\) and \(\mu^\circ(\text{Ag}) = 0\), we get  
\[
\mu^\circ
